title: Auto-route to installed MCP tools
story: As a framework user, I want agents to automatically use my installed MCP tools instead of lower-fidelity built-in tools, so that I get better results without manually intervening.
acceptance_criteria: When I have code-index MCP installed and an agent tries Grep, the hook blocks and redirects to the MCP search tool
When I have bulk-fs MCP installed and an agent tries Read for a full file, the hook warns about the MCP alternative
When no MCP tools are available, all built-in tools work normally

title: Configure custom tool preferences
story: As a framework user, I want to define my own tool routing rules in a config file, so that my specific tool preferences are enforced without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: I can add a new rule to tool-routing.json and it takes effect on the next tool call
My custom rules override framework defaults
The config file format is documented and easy to understand

title: Legitimate tool use not blocked
story: As a framework user, I want my legitimate use of Grep/Glob/Read (targeted file reads for editing, specific file searches) to not be blocked, so that the hook doesn't create false positives.
acceptance_criteria: Reading a file with a limit parameter for edit prep is not blocked
Grep targeting a specific file path is not blocked
Glob with an exact filename is not blocked

title: Discover config path from warnings
story: As a framework user, when I see a tool routing warning, I want to know where to edit the config to change the enforcement level, so that I can promote warn to block easily.
acceptance_criteria: Warning messages include the full path to tool-routing.json
Warning messages name the preferred tool and the rule source

title: Skill-declared tool preferences enforced
story: As a skill author, I want to declare tool preferences in my skill manifest, so that when users install my skill, the preferred tools are automatically enforced.
acceptance_criteria: Adding tool_preferences to a skill's bindings in the manifest makes those preferences active
Skill-declared preferences are enforced at block level

title: Audit trail for tool routing decisions
story: As a framework user, I want a log of all tool routing decisions, so that I can audit which tools were used and which were redirected.
acceptance_criteria: Every routing decision is logged to .isdlc/tool-routing-audit.jsonl
Each log entry includes timestamp, tools, enforcement, decision, and rule source
Audit log failures don't block tool execution
